body.title: Vetinari: tag-based team routing — CC/Librarian/DickSimnel
body.intention: I intend that Vetinari maps each decomposed subtask to the correct worker (CC for build, Librarian for research, DickSimnel for token-light) based on ticket tags.
body.description:
decompose_directive() currently writes tickets but assigns no worker. The vision requires routing to CC (build), Librarian (research), DickSimnel (token-light) based on work type.

Add _route_worker(tags: list[str]) -> str to VetinariDevice: ROUTING_TABLE maps tag patterns to worker names (Build/Code/Architecture → 'claude'; Research/Memory/Reading → 'librarian'; Review/Check/Summarize → 'dicksimnel'; default → 'claude'). Wire into decompose_directive(): set worker field on each ticket before cc_queue write.

**Affected files:** devices/vetinari/device.py (add _route_worker(), ROUTING_TABLE), tests/test_vetinari_dispatch.py (NEW)
**Design rules:** routing table as module-level constant (not config file) — simple and auditable; worker names must match cc_queue.py worker field values
**Scope boundary:** IN — ROUTING_TABLE, _route_worker(), wiring into decompose; OUT — Granny-level dispatch protocol, DickSimnel connection setup
**Completion criteria:** _route_worker(['Build', 'Architecture']) == 'claude'; _route_worker(['Research']) == 'librarian'; _route_worker(['Summarize']) == 'dicksimnel'; _route_worker([]) == 'claude'; tickets written by decompose_directive() carry correct worker field; 4+ tests
